title: End-to-end lifecycle — order-triage AgentCore
subtitle: One frame, two tracks: a Build & deploy track (B1-B6) ships a gated agent image into the runtime spine (Request track R1-R7); telemetry and online evals (grey) close the loop back to the knowledge corpus
desc: Combined whole-lifecycle view of the order-triage AgentCore mono-repo, fusing all nine planes. TOP tributary (Build & deploy, B1-B6): the knowledge corpus (ontology · skills · KB docs) and mono-repo flow through path-filtered CI and the agent-build / stubs-release publishers, past the offline eval gate, into ECR + S3 artifacts, then a human-gated, OIDC-auth terraform apply. The apply provisions the MIDDLE runtime spine (Request track R1-R7): an Entra-authenticated analyst invokes the AgentCore Runtime (CUSTOM_JWT) on Nova Lite behind a Guardrail, with LOCAL knowledge tools, a Knowledge Base, and per-user short/long-term Memory, through the Cedar-guarded MCP Gateway out to the stub Lambdas (SigV4 for sap/orders, Entra-OBO for snowflake) and into Snowflake as the human under RLS. BOTTOM tributary (Observe): runtime telemetry lands in X-Ray + CloudWatch (dashboards, alarms, SLOs); AgentCore Online Evaluations grade the gen_ai spans and feed findings back to the corpus.
notes: Combined hero (replaces the simplified system-overview): spine + two tributaries + one return edge, fusing all nine planes into one lifecycle. A wide vertical gutter separates the Build & deploy band (top, y100-360) from the runtime spine (y600-1110) so the AWS Cloud zone border and the crossing identity edges have clear air. Build & deploy track B1-B6 runs left-to-right across the top then drops a single 'provisions' arrow (B6) into the Runtime. Request track R1-R7 is the data-plane spine (Cedar ENFORCE explicit, the SigV4-vs-OBO 6a/6b/6c egress split, Snowflake EXTERNAL_OAUTH+RLS). Knowledge appears as the corpus (build) + LOCAL tools + Knowledge Base (runtime); Memory is split short/long-term; Evaluation has two touchpoints — the offline gate inside the build and Online Evaluations grading live gen_ai spans. Observability (X-Ray, CW Logs, dashboards, alarms, SLOs) is the grey bottom strip; telemetry edges are unnumbered. The lone grey return edge onlineeval->ontology closes the lifecycle loop without forcing a numbered cycle. Snowflake sits outside the AWS Cloud boundary (external EXTERNAL_OAUTH egress). Coordinates calibrated for zero overlap; regenerate via the architecture-skill — never hand-edit the SVG.

title: Security architecture — order-triage AgentCore
subtitle: One InvokeAgentRuntime call as an identity: Entra CUSTOM_JWT in · Cedar at the Gateway · SigV4 (agent) vs Entra-OBO (user) egress split · Snowflake RLS
desc: Left-to-right security view: an analyst signs into Microsoft Entra via the webapp and invokes the AgentCore Runtime with the user JWT (CUSTOM_JWT). The Runtime streams to Bedrock Nova Lite behind a prompt-attack Guardrail, partitions memory by the Entra subject, and forwards the JWT to the AgentCore Gateway, which authorizes every MCP call with Cedar. Egress splits: SAP and order-actions Lambdas are called with the agent's SigV4 identity, while the Snowflake-query Lambda receives a per-user Entra OBO token, so Snowflake runs the query as the human under row-level security.

edges.label: OAUTH → run as the user · RLS

title: Agent reasoning plane — order-triage AgentCore
subtitle: One InvokeAgentRuntime turn inside the CUSTOM_JWT gate: Strands event loop drives Nova Lite (+Guardrail), LOCAL knowledge tools, the Cedar-guarded MCP Gateway, and per-user Memory
desc: Per-turn reasoning loop of the order-triage AgentCore agent: caller -> AgentCore Runtime (CUSTOM_JWT) -> Strands Agent event loop, which calls Bedrock Nova Lite (+Guardrail), LOCAL tools over the three knowledge surfaces (Knowledge Base -> S3 Vectors, ontology + skills corpus), the AgentCore Gateway (MCP, Cedar ENFORCE) to backend Lambdas, and reads/writes AgentCore Memory.
notes: Per-turn reasoning loop only (steps 1-8); cold-start assembly (build_agent steps 1-6) and the identity/OBO/Snowflake-RLS data plane are separate diagrams. Cedar folded into the Gateway label. Backend Lambdas + Snowflake collapsed to one out-of-scope node. Guardrail shown as an async id-edge off the model (PROMPT_ATTACK-only, default-on when both id+version env vars set). LOCAL tools never traverse the Gateway; S3 Vectors sits just outside that dashed zone, reached from the Knowledge Base by a supporting vector-search edge. Memory is partitioned per actor_id and active only when session_id is non-null. 5a/5b and 7a/7b are the parallel tool-call / tool-result fan-out of one event-loop cycle.

title: Memory architecture — order-triage AgentCore
subtitle: Entra sub = actor_id partition key · async WRITE (CreateEvent -> short-term -> extract 3 strategies) vs per-turn READ (RetrieveMemoryRecords -> threshold -> <user_context>)
desc: The per-user memory plane: the Entra subject becomes actor_id; the AgentCore Runtime writes short-term events to AgentCore Memory every turn (async), an extraction pipeline populates three long-term strategies (SEMANTIC/facts, USER_PREFERENCE/preferences, SUMMARIZATION/summaries) keyed per actor_id, and each user turn retrieves and prepends a user_context block to the model prompt.
notes: Faithful to memory-architecture.md + ADR-0002. WRITE path (steps 3-6c, thick req): Strands Agent -> AgentCore Memory CreateEvent every turn -> short-term event store (90d) -> async model-backed extraction (memory_execution_role, only bedrock:InvokeModel) -> the three long-term strategies (SEMANTIC /facts/{actorId}, USER_PREFERENCE /preferences/{actorId}, SUMMARIZATION /summaries/{actorId}/{sessionId}), all keyed per actor_id. READ path (steps 7-9, thick req): on a user MessageAddedEvent, RetrieveMemoryRecords (~3/turn, top_k 5/5/3, score floor 0.3) -> concat hits -> prepend <user_context> to the latest user message (message-level, not system prompt). actor_id is the Entra sub (sub->oid->ANONYMOUS), read off the contextvar by the session manager (not via build_agent) — shown as the blue identity partition-key edge across the top. In-session short-term replay and the per-namespace searches are grey supporting edges. Observability control plane (native RetrieveMemoryRecords span, ADOT boto3 span, vended logs/X-Ray) is intentionally folded into prose, not drawn, to keep the data-plane WRITE-vs-READ split legible. Validated by rendering with _awsviz.render + qlmanage; no node/label overlaps at the chosen coordinates.

title: Observability architecture — order-triage AgentCore (control plane)
subtitle: Source (telemetry emitters) → Routing + Enrichment (log groups · X-Ray · evaluations) → Destination (governance / operations dashboards · anomaly alarms → SNS → email). Off the InvokeAgentRuntime request path.
desc: Grey control plane of the order-triage AgentCore system. Two independent emission mechanisms — ADOT gen_ai.* spans to X-Ray Transaction Search (aws/spans), and a hand-rolled per-turn EMF token metric printed to the UNMASKED runtime -DEFAULT log group and auto-extracted into the OrderTriage/Agent namespace — feed per-resource APPLICATION_LOGS / USAGE_LOGS / TRACES delivery trios plus the MASKED Bedrock model-invocation log. Downstream: 4 audience dashboards, the static+anomaly alarms rolling into a composite to SNS and email, Contributor Insights, App-Signals SLOs, and opt-in online Evaluations.

groups.label: Routing + Enrichment — log groups · X-Ray · evaluations

notes: Mirrors the Source→Routing+Enrichment→Destination control-plane flow of the user's reference; faithful to observability-architecture.md (EMF token metric, ADOT gen_ai spans → X-Ray, vended logs, dashboards/alarms/SNS).

title: Evaluation architecture — order-triage AgentCore
subtitle: Two surfaces grade one agent: OFFLINE pytest gate (deterministic + Anthropic judge) blocks the image · ONLINE AgentCore Evaluations sample the shared gen_ai trace substrate (scoring BLOCKED on AgentSpanMappingException)
desc: The evaluation plane of the order-triage AgentCore system, shown left-to-right as two lanes that grade the same order_triage Strands agent over a shared gen_ai trace substrate. OFFLINE (left): a developer/CI pytest gate runs the real agent over cases.yaml, applies deterministic tool/substring checks then an Anthropic LLM-as-judge, and a green gate publishes the agent image to ECR which deploys to the runtime. SHARED (centre): the AgentCore Runtime emits gen_ai spans (aws/spans + -DEFAULT content). ONLINE (right): AgentCore Online Evaluations sample those traces at 100%, run 5 built-in evaluators whose InvokeModel judge currently fails with AgentSpanMappingException (0 scores), with result records and the Operations dashboard eval panel as sinks.

groups.label: OFFLINE — CI eval gate (agent/evals) · GATES deploy
ONLINE — AgentCore Online Evaluations · CONTINUOUS · default-off

notes: Two dashed lanes share the SHARED runtime substrate: OFFLINE gate (numbered 1-8) on the left, ONLINE (A-E) on the right. Faithful to evaluation-architecture.md: the offline pytest gate runs the REAL agent over cases.yaml (deterministic checks then Anthropic judge) and a green gate publishes the image to ECR. The runtime emits gen_ai spans both surfaces read. Online scoring is BLOCKED at AgentSpanMappingException (edge D, alarm icon, 0 scores DEFERRED). The unverified metric-namespace path is folded into prose, not drawn; the Results log group is shown as the real sink, with the dashboard banner reflecting the blocker. The cross-lane "tool spans → Tool accuracy" edge is routed above the group labels so it does not collide with them.

title: Knowledge architecture — order-triage AgentCore
subtitle: How the enterprise ontology (Objects + Actions), Skills and KB are structured and consumed: three LOCAL knowledge tools read the baked-in corpus; one Action binds to a Cedar-guarded Gateway MCP tool
desc: Structural knowledge plane of the order-triage AgentCore agent: the knowledge corpus (42-object ontology + actions, skills, KB policy docs, compiled to a bindings.json reverse index) is baked into the agent image and consumed through three LOCAL tools — describe_entity (ontology), load_skill (skills), search_policies (Bedrock Knowledge Base over S3 Vectors). Ontology Actions bind to AgentCore Gateway MCP tools via ACTION_IMPLEMENTATIONS (Cedar ENFORCE), egressing to backend Lambdas.
notes: Structural cut (asset relationships + consumption), not the per-turn loop (agent plane) or identity/OBO internals (security plane). The three knowledge surfaces map 1:1 to the three LOCAL tools; all are in-process and never traverse the Gateway. Corpus assets reference each other by stable apiName (Actions target Objects; Skills applyTo/read Objects + invoke Actions; KB chunks concern Objects) and compile to bindings.json (apiName -> skills/kb/actions). The single blue-dashed edge actions->Gateway is ACTION_IMPLEMENTATIONS ({raiseException: orders___flagOrder}), asserted by the startup coverage gate — the ontology declares the what, the agent binds the how (ADR-0001). KB docs are ingested (S3 sync -> Titan v2 embed) into the Bedrock KB; search_policies does a k=3 retrieve. The snowflake backend is one Cortex Analyst /ask over ORDERS_SV (ADR-0008). Only raiseException has a Gateway implementation today; the other two MCP tools (sap getCreditStatus, snowflake ask) are reads.

title: Detailed data plane — order-triage AgentCore
subtitle: The full request/data flow of one InvokeAgentRuntime call: Entra CUSTOM_JWT in → Strands on Nova Lite → Cedar ENFORCE at the MCP Gateway → SigV4 (agent) vs Entra-OBO (user) egress → Snowflake EXTERNAL_OAUTH + RLS as the calling human
desc: Detailed sibling of the system overview: same whole-system scope but expands the identity / authorization / OBO / RLS chain. One InvokeAgentRuntime call from an Entra-authenticated caller, through the AgentCore Runtime (CUSTOM_JWT) and the Cedar-guarded MCP Gateway, out to the stub Lambdas (SigV4 for sap/orders, Entra-OBO Bearer for snowflake), and into Snowflake as the signed-in human (EXTERNAL_OAUTH + RLS). Grey edges are the observability control plane.
notes: Detailed/annotated sibling of system-overview (reuses its layout): adds the explicit Cedar ENFORCE node, numbers the request path 1-6, and spells out the identity/OBO/RLS chain in the labels. Egress split: sap/orders SigV4 (agent identity), snowflake Entra-OBO Bearer (the signed-in user). snowflake-query Lambda has two auth paths (user OBO | service KEYPAIR_JWT, in its sub); the order-actions->snowflake status check and the Snowflake EXTERNAL_OAUTH upn->user/scp->role mapping are in the Wire-level Mermaid. snowflake/Snowflake is external (ap-southeast-1); Observability is the grey control plane.

title: DevOps / CI-CD architecture — order-triage AgentCore
subtitle: Single-repo, path-filtered pipeline: a merge to main routes by folder to per-folder CI + the agent-build / stubs-release publishers (ECR image · S3 zips/KB), which repository_dispatch into a human-gated, OIDC-auth terraform apply
desc: Delivery plane of the mono-repo: a push/merge to aniryou/bedrock-demo triggers path-filtered GitHub Actions — per-folder *-ci validation, agent-build (bakes in-tree knowledge -> ARM64 image to ECR + KB docs to S3) on agent/ or knowledge/ changes, and stubs-release (Lambda zips + OpenAPI to S3) on stubs/ changes. The publishers repository_dispatch (agent-image-published / stubs-published) into the gated deploy.yml, which blocks on a manual approval, assumes the deploy OIDC role (env:production), runs preflight, and terraform apply's against S3 remote state into the live AgentCore stack.

nodes.title: Developer
nodes.sub: git push · PR · merge to main
nodes.label: below

notes: Mono-repo replacement for the retired 5-repo repository_dispatch cascade: ONE repo with folders, path filters route a change to the right workflow. Knowledge is baked in-tree — agent-build triggers on BOTH agent/** and knowledge/** and runs `make skills` over the local ../knowledge (no cross-repo fetch, no SKILLS_TOKEN). The two publishers still repository_dispatch into deploy.yml, but intra-repo (stubs-release uses a PAT because GITHUB_TOKEN dispatches don't start new runs). Deploy is human-gated (trstringer/manual-approval + environment:production) BEFORE any AWS creds; OIDC trust pinned to repo:aniryou/bedrock-demo:environment:production. Dispatch edges use distinct source anchors + outer lanes (high for agent, low for stubs) so each origin is traceable; the CI PR-validate lane is grey/supporting (no artifact).
